# Nafplio Car Rental — Full Content Reference


---

# Homepage

URL: https://nafplio-car-rental.com/

# Nafplio Car Rental — Compare Local Prices, Skip Athens Traffic

Nafplio, the First Capital of Greece, sits in Argolis in the Peloponnese, 169 km from Athens International Airport (ATH). ELSTAT recorded 297,320 arrivals in 2024, and the town’s road access makes car hire the most flexible option for Mycenae, Epidaurus, Tolo, and Porto Heli. This page compares local agencies such as Staikos Rent a Car, Bounos Car Rental, Makris Car Rental, Alma Car Rental, Base Rent a Car, Euro Rent a Car, Imperial Car Rental, Hermes Travel, Stamou Car Rental, Aspida Car Rental, G.A. Car Rental, and Ride & Drive, plus brokers that may subcontract to those fleets. A complete [Athens airport to Nafplio drive guide](/athens-airport-to-nafplio) is available if you are planning the route from Athens.

## How Much Does a Rental Car Cost in Nafplio?

A Nafplio car rental typically starts at €18/day for a broker-listed Ford Fiesta and rises to €27/day for an automatic Skoda Octavia or €40/day for a Kia Sportage, but those prices are published broker rates and not a confirmed local-agency quote. Seasonal pricing shifts materially: Stressfreecarrental and Skyscanner commonly show €40–€43/day in April, May, September, and October, while summer demand in August can push walk-up rates above €300/day at major brands. Momondo also shows December as the cheapest month for car rental in Nafplio.

| Category | Off-season (Nov–Mar) | High-season (Jul–Aug) |
| --- | ---: | ---: |
| Mini/Economy manual | €18–€28/day | €40–€60/day |
| Compact automatic | €27–€35/day | €55–€80/day |
| Family SUV | €40–€55/day | €80–€120/day |

Insurance terms matter because quotes are often incomplete. In Greece, CDW usually carries an excess/deductible or franchise of €600–€1,200, while SCDW or FDW can reduce that to zero excess or full coverage for an extra €8–€15/day; WUG is often sold as an add-on for wheels, underside, and glass. All rentals must still include statutory third-party liability under Bank of Greece Act 195/3/29.11.2021, so a quote that omits that base cover is not compliant.

> 💡 **Tip:** Ask each agency to confirm, in writing, whether the quote includes CDW, SCDW, FDW, WUG, and the size of the excess/deductible before you preauthorise the card. Nafplio agencies often use different wording for the same coverage, so the booking voucher should name the insurer and the franchise amount.

## Where to Pick Up — Athens Airport, Nafplio Waterfront, or Cruise Pier?

A Nafplio pickup can be faster and cheaper than an Athens one, but the right choice depends on tolls, transfer time, and your arrival point. Athens Airport to Nafplio is about 169 km and roughly 2 hours 5 minutes by car via Olympia Odos, Moreas A7, the Korinthos interchange, and the EO Argous–Nafpliou approach, with a toll range of €4.60–€7.55 depending on whether you add Spathovouni for a Nemea detour. The fuel cost at a €2.062/L benchmark puts the total one-way drive near €36–€40.

| Route element | Value |
| --- | ---: |
| Elefsina toll | €2.50 |
| Isthmos toll | €2.10 |
| Spathovouni toll | €2.95 |
| Total toll range | €4.60–€7.55 |
| Drive time Athens Airport to Nafplio | ~2 h 5 min |

**1. Athens Airport (ATH) Drive:** Collecting at Athens Airport gives full route control through Elefsina, Isthmos, and the Moreas A7 corridor, but it also exposes you to Athens traffic and potential delays on Attiki Odos before the airport exit. For a more detailed route breakdown, see [Athens airport drive directions](/athens-airport-to-nafplio).  
> ⚠️ **Warning:** If you plan a one-way pickup in Athens and a drop-off in Nafplio, confirm the rental contract allows intercity travel and that the insurance remains valid on toll motorways; the written voucher should mention CDW or FDW explicitly.

**2. KTEL Bus + Waterfront Pickup:** KTEL Argolidas runs from Kifissou terminal to Syngrou 8 in Nafplio, and the waterfront office strip near Akti Miaouli, Bouboulinas, and Syngrou 8 is typically reachable on foot from the bus station in about 200 meters. A public timetable reference from KTEL Argolidas and local office postings shows services roughly every two hours from 05:00 to 21:30, with fares usually in the €11–€19 range. This option suits travelers who want to avoid Athens traffic and start the rental only after arriving in town.  
> 💡 **Tip:** If you arrive by bus, ask for delivery to Syngrou 8 or Akti Miaouli rather than a hotel handoff, because local agencies such as Staikos Rent a Car and Bounos Car Rental are clustered on the waterfront.

**3. Cruise Tender Port:** Cruise passengers arrive by tender to the Nafplio waterfront, and the walk from the pier to Staikos on Bouboulinas or Bounos near A. Syngrou is typically 150–300 meters. Open Top Bus Hellas M.E.P.E. offers a sightseeing alternative at about €8 for adults and €5 for children on a 45-minute loop, which can be useful if your ship stay is only six hours. For short stays, a half-day rental must be negotiated directly because many Nafplio agencies do not publish half-day rates online.  
> ⚠️ **Warning:** Cruise schedules can compress your rental window, so confirm the exact pickup time and return deadline in writing; a late return can trigger extra day charges even when the rental is only a few hours long.

## Local Agencies vs International Brokers — Who's Who in Nafplio

Nafplio has 12 named local agencies that regularly appear in local search results and broker fulfillment records: Staikos Rent a Car, Bounos Car Rental, Makris Car Rental, Alma Car Rental, Base Rent a Car, Euro Rent a Car, Imperial Car Rental, Hermes Travel, Stamou Car Rental, Aspida Car Rental, G.A. Car Rental, and Ride & Drive. International brokers such as Auto Europe, Kayak, Booking.com, Stressfreecarrental, and Skyscanner may subcontract the vehicle to one of those operators, so the local supplier name matters on the voucher.

**Staikos Rent a Car:** Staikos Rent a Car, owned by Christos Bouchoutsos, operates from the waterfront area near Bouboulinas and Syngrou 8 and is one of the longest-running Nafplio agencies, with a business history dating back to the late 1970s; its fleet commonly includes Peugeot, Citroen, Fiat, Audi, Mercedes, Ford Fiesta, Hyundai i10, and Toyota Aygo.

**G.A. Car Rental:** G.A. Car Rental is managed by Mr Gavriil, and local listings identify the owner-run business as one of the established Nafplio suppliers; the fleet mix commonly includes compact cars for Argolis day trips and is often used by visitors heading to Mycenae and Epidaurus.

**Makris Car Rental:** Makris Car Rental has operated since 1986 and keeps an office at 25is Martiou 12 plus a second branch in Nea Kios, which is useful for routes toward Tiryns and the EO Argous–Nafpliou corridor; Makris is notable for publicly advertising snow-chain rental as an extra.

**Alma Car Rental:** Alma Car Rental appears in local booking searches with compact Peugeot and Citroen models and is often discussed in forums alongside waterfront pickup convenience; the company is one of the local names that can be compared against broker quotes for a same-day pickup.

**Base Rent a Car:** Base Rent a Car, listed at nafpliorentacar.gr, is part of the local supply chain that may fulfill broker bookings and typically competes on short-stay Nafplio rentals rather than airport transfers.

**Euro Rent a Car:** Euro Rent a Car, operating via eurorentacar.eu since 1986, is one of the older local names and can appear in broker-fulfilled reservations for travelers arriving by bus or cruise tender.

**Imperial Car Rental:** Imperial Car Rental publishes broker rates such as a Ford Fiesta from €18/day, an automatic Skoda Octavia from €27/day, and a Kia Sportage from €40/day, but the published rate should be treated as an unverified broker listing unless the final voucher names a Nafplio office.

**Hermes Travel:** Hermes Travel is one of the local Nafplio operators that can be used for mixed transport and rental arrangements, especially when a transfer and car handoff are combined in the same booking.

**Stamou Car Rental:** Stamou Car Rental appears among the Nafplio fleet names and is part of the local comparison set for visitors who want to avoid airport pickup fees and use a waterfront handoff.

**Aspida Car Rental:** Aspida Car Rental is another local option in the Nafplio market, and its value depends on whether the booking includes a proper insurance package and a clear pickup address.

**Ride & Drive:** Ride & Drive publishes a transparent €20 surcharge for 21:00–08:00 pickups, which is useful for late ferry, late bus, or delayed flight arrivals.

> 💡 **Tip:** If you need the cleanest pickup logistics, compare the voucher address against Bouboulinas, Syngrou 8, and Akti Miaouli, because those streets place you closest to the KTEL Argolidas stop and the waterfront offices. Once you have the keys, our [Old Town parking and plate-removal guide](/nafplio-old-town-parking) explains where to leave the car safely.

## What You Can Do With a Rental Car in 48 Hours

Nafplio is a practical base for a 48-hour Peloponnese itinerary because the main archaeological sites in Argolis are close enough for half-day driving loops. ELSTAT visitor patterns and local tour data show that many stays are short, so distances and ticket prices matter more than generic sightseeing claims.

| Place | Drive from Nafplio | Ticket | Notes |
| --- | ---: | ---: | --- |
| Mycenae | 21 km / 29 min | €20 | Main site, museum, Treasury of Atreus |
| Epidaurus | 26 km / 31 min | €20 | Theatre, sanctuary, museum |
| Tiryns | 4 km / 10 min | €10 | Cyclopean walls, small parking area |
| Karathona Beach | 5 km / 10 min | Free access | Blue Flag withdrawn July 2025 |
| Nemea | 50 km / 50 min | Varies | Wine region via Moreas A7 |
| Tolo | 10 km / 15 min | Beach access | Family beach village |
| Porto Heli | 76 km / 90 min | Varies | Ferry access toward Spetses |

**Mycenae:** The Hellenic Ministry of Culture via odysseus.culture.gr lists Mycenae as a UNESCO-linked archaeological site, and the 2026 ticket price is €20 for the main complex, museum, and Treasury of Atreus; summer hours begin at 08:00 and extend to 20:00 from 02.05.2026.

**Epidaurus:** The same ministry source lists Epidaurus at €20 for the theatre, sanctuary, and museum, with 08:00–20:00 summer hours and a 31-minute drive from Nafplio via the Argolis road network.

**Tiryns:** Tiryns sits only 4 km from Nafplio, the ticket is €10, and the parking lot is directly off the Argos–Nafplio road; the short drive makes it the easiest archaeological stop for a same-morning visit.

**Karathona Beach:** Karathona Beach is no longer a Blue Flag beach, because the Hellenic Society for the Protection of Nature withdrew the award in July 2025; water quality can still be good, but the certification is not current for 2026.

**Nemea Wine Region:** Nemea is reached in about 50 minutes through the A7 Moreas corridor, and drivers should pay attention at the Nemea exit and Spathovouni-related routing if they are combining wine tasting with motorway travel.

**Tolo and Porto Heli:** Tolo is a 10 km seaside village, while Porto Heli is a 76 km drive and a gateway to ferries for Spetses; both can be covered in the same rental itinerary if you start early from Nafplio.

> 💡 **Tip:** For a one-day archaeological loop, combine Mycenae and Epidaurus first, then return through Tiryns, because that sequence keeps the driving inside Argolis and limits backtracking on the EO Argous–Nafpliou road. Our full [Mycenae and Epidaurus by-car routing](/mycenae-epidaurus-by-car) lays out the two-day split with ticket prices and free parking notes for each site.

## What Changed in 2026 — The New Greek Traffic Code

Greece’s current road code is Law 5209/2025, which replaced the older K.O.K. framework on 13 September 2025 and was amended by Law 5290/2026 on 30 March 2026. The update matters to anyone driving a rental in Nafplio because traffic fines, license suspensions, and insurance consequences now follow the driver more closely than before.

| Offense | Article | Fine | Additional penalty |
| --- | ---: | ---: | --- |
| Mobile phone use while driving | Article 17 | €350 | 30-day license suspension and plate seizure |
| Drink-driving 0.50–0.80 g/L BAC | Article 46 | €350 | 30-day license suspension |
| No seatbelt | Article 16 | €350 | 30-day license suspension |
| Speeding 30–50 km/h over limit | Article 22 | €350 | 30-day license and plate suspension |

> ⚠️ **Warning:** A BAC above 0.50 g/L can void CDW, SCDW, FDW, and any zero excess or full coverage add-on, so the renter can become personally liable for damage even when the rental contract looked comprehensive. The Hellenic Police enforce the road code, and foreign drivers can face penalties in Greece even when the car belongs to a local Nafplio agency.

> 💡 **Tip:** For disputed citations in Nafplio, contact Tmima Trochaias Tourismou Nafpliou at ttournafpliou@astynomia.gr or the local police office at Eleftherias 1-2, 21100 Nafplio; keep the notice number and the rental voucher because the municipality and the police may both request them.

## Why This Site

This site compares named Nafplio suppliers, broker rates, toll routes, local roads, and site-to-site driving times instead of generic destination copy. The data points below are the ones travelers usually need first, and each item is tied to a named source or address.

* KTEL Argolidas times and fares matter for bus-to-waterfront arrivals, especially when the pickup address is Syngrou 8 or Bouboulinas.
* Open Top Bus Hellas M.E.P.E. gives a fallback for short cruise calls when a half-day rental is not practical.
* Makris Car Rental is the only named local agency in this page that publicly advertises snow-chain rental.
* ELSTAT arrival data, the Hellenic Ministry of Culture, Bank of Greece rules, and the Hellenic Police road code all shape the practical rental decision.

> 💡 **Tip:** If your priority is the cheapest possible pickup, compare local offices near the KTEL Argolidas stop before accepting an Athens Airport contract, because the tolls on Olympia Odos and Moreas A7 can erase a small daily-rate saving.

# Athens Airport (ATH) to Nafplio

URL: https://nafplio-car-rental.com/nafplio-airport-car-rental

**PAGE TITLE:** Athens Airport to Nafplio — Drive, Tolls & KTEL Bus Alternative  
**SLUG:** `/athens-airport-to-nafplio`

# Athens Airport (ATH) to Nafplio: Drive, Tolls, and KTEL Bus Alternative

Athens Airport to Nafplio is a 169 km transfer that typically takes about 2 hours and 5 minutes by car, with one-way tolls of €4.60 to €7.55 for a standard passenger car depending on whether you exit at Korinthos interchange or continue via the Moreas A7 and Spathovouni. The route uses Attiki Odos, Olympia Odos, and the EO Argous–Nafpliou approach into Argolis, while the KTEL Argolidas bus from Syngrou 8 is the main low-cost alternative for travelers who want to collect a car in Nafplio instead of Athens. For parking and final arrival details, see our [Nafplio old-town parking guide](/nafplio-old-town-parking); the [2026 Greek traffic-code rules in force from 13 September 2025](/nafplio-driving-rules-2026) cover speed limits, BAC and the new fine schedule before you set off.

## Route 1 — Drive Direct from Athens Airport to Nafplio

Driving from Athens International Airport to Nafplio is the most flexible choice for travelers who want a car immediately after landing and plan to explore the Peloponnese beyond Nafplio. The route passes through Attiki Odos, Olympia Odos, and Moreas A7 before the final EO Argous–Nafpliou section into Argolis.

**Athens Airport to Nafplio driving sequence:**

1. **Athens Airport exit:** Follow signs for Attiki Odos toward Elefsina, then merge onto the main motorway network.
2. **Elefsina to Corinth:** Continue on Olympia Odos to the Elefsina toll plaza and the Isthmos toll plaza near the Corinth Canal.
3. **Korinthos interchange:** At the Korinthos interchange, choose either the cheaper national-road exit or the faster Moreas A7 continuation.
4. **Nemea option:** If you continue on Moreas A7, the Spathovouni toll applies before the exit toward Nemea and Nafplio.
5. **Final approach:** After the motorway, follow signs for Argos and Nafplio on EO Argous–Nafpliou into the town center.

> 💡 **Tip:** For navigation, use “Nafplio” or “Ναύπλιο” in your GPS rather than relying only on the airport name, because many rental-car systems display the route as a chain of motorway names rather than the final destination.

**Athens to Nafplio tolls for a standard car**

| Toll plaza | Route | Passenger car toll | Notes |
| --- | --- | ---: | --- |
| Elefsina | Olympia Odos | €2.50 | Main westbound toll after Attiki Odos. |
| Isthmos | Olympia Odos | €2.10 | Passed after the Corinth Canal area. |
| Spathovouni | Moreas A7 | €2.95 | Applies only if you continue via Nemea. |
| Total via national-road exit | Olympia Odos + exit at Korinthos interchange | €4.60 | Lower-cost choice. |
| Total via Moreas A7 | Olympia Odos + Moreas A7 | €7.55 | Faster motorway choice. |

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Toll pricing should be checked against the official operator before departure, because motorway tariffs can change and preauthorisation on some rental cards may temporarily hold extra funds even when the final toll spend is low.

**Fuel and distance benchmark:** Athens Airport to Nafplio is about 169 km, and at 7.5 L/100 km with Greek Euro 95 at roughly €2.062 per litre, the fuel spend is about €26 for the one-way drive. That makes the total road cost roughly €30 to €34 before the rental-day charge, according to an April 2026 fuel snapshot and the published motorway tariff tables.

**Traffic and safety note:** The final motorway exit toward Nafplio can carry a higher accident risk near the Nemea approach, so drivers should reduce speed well before the ramp and avoid last-second lane changes.

## Route 2 — KTEL Bus + Nafplio Waterfront Car Pickup

KTEL Argolidas is the best alternative for travelers who prefer to avoid Athens airport traffic and collect a rental car in Nafplio rather than paying airport location surcharges. The bus terminal at Syngrou 8 places the waterfront agencies within a short walk, including Staikos Rent a Car and Bounos Car Rental.

**KTEL Argolidas bus facts:**

| Item | Value | Source cue |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Departure point | Kifissou Intercity Bus Terminal, Athens | KTEL Argolidas |
| Arrival point | Nafplio terminal, Syngrou 8 | KTEL Argolidas |
| Journey time | About 2 hours and 10 minutes | Published timetable pattern |
| One-way fare | €11 to €19 | Seasonal fare range |
| Typical frequency | About every 2 hours | Daytime service pattern |

The KTEL bus can be a practical transfer if your plan is to rent locally in Nafplio from agencies on Bouboulinas, Syngrou, or the surrounding waterfront streets. Staikos Rent a Car is associated with rentacarnafplio.gr and owner Christos Bouchoutsos, while Bounos Car Rental is listed on bounos-carrental.com near the terminal area.

**Walking times from Syngrou 8 to waterfront agencies**

| Agency | Address clue | Walk from terminal |
| --- | --- | ---: |
| Staikos Rent a Car | Bouboulinas 50 | 200 m, about 3 min |
| Bounos Car Rental | A. Syngrou 14 | 100 m, about 1 min |
| Euro Rent a Car | Polizoidou 8 | 350 m, about 5 min |
| Hermes Travel | Old Town edge | 400 m, about 6 min |

> 💡 **Tip:** If you are traveling in August, reserve the KTEL Argolidas ticket in advance and compare it with a local pickup quote from Base Rent a Car or Alma Car Rental before deciding on the airport pick-up.

## Route 3 — Cruise Tender Pier Pickup in Nafplio

Nafplio works well for cruise passengers because the tender pier drops visitors near the waterfront, where several rental agencies and the Open Top Bus Hellas M.E.P.E. departure point are within a short walking radius. That layout is useful for a half-day hire, a port-day loop, or a short transfer toward Karathona Beach.

**Cruise-day pickup points and local access:**

| Place | Distance from tender area | Practical use |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Staikos Rent a Car | About 150–300 m | Fast pickup for waterfront arrivals |
| Bounos Car Rental | About 150–300 m | Short walk from the pier |
| Open Top Bus Hellas M.E.P.E. | Near Boumpoulinas 41 | Sightseeing alternative |
| Bourtzi viewpoint | Waterfront stop | Short scenic walk |
| Bouboulinas promenade | Central approach | Easy navigation reference |

For short port calls, many travelers choose a local rental from Staikos, Makris Car Rental, Imperial Car Rental, or Ride & Drive rather than driving deep into the Argolis prefecture. If your itinerary includes Mycenae, Epidaurus, or Tiryns, a same-day rental is workable only when the vessel schedule leaves enough buffer for return and boarding.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Do not assume a 6-hour cruise stop is enough for Mycenae or Epidaurus if you also need boarding clearance and return-buffer time, because a late return can trigger extra rental charges and pier-area enforcement issues.

## Which Nafplio Pickup Option Fits Your Trip?

Nafplio pickup works best when your itinerary is centered on Argolis, the Peloponnese, or a short waterfront stay rather than a late-night arrival at Athens Airport. Athens Airport pickup works better when the car is needed immediately for wider regional driving, including Nemea, Porto Heli, or a longer loop through the Peloponnese.

**Best-fit scenarios by traveler type:**

- 🚗 **Airport pickup** — best if you land after 21:30 or need a car for an immediate trip beyond Nafplio.
- 🚌 **KTEL pickup** — best if you want to save on tolls and collect a car near Syngrou 8.
- 🛳️ **Cruise pickup** — best if your port call is short and your route stays close to Nafplio.

**Local points of interest that influence the decision:**

| Place | Why it matters | Distance context |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Palamidi Fortress | Steep access by car or foot | Central Nafplio landmark |
| Akronafplia | Old-town hill access | Useful for parking choices |
| Karathona Beach | Beach transfer by car | Road access south of town |
| Tolo | Popular base for coastal stays | Short drive from Nafplio |
| Mycenae and Epidaurus | Day-trip demand | Better with an airport or local car |

> 💡 **Tip:** If your trip includes Palamidi Fortress, Bourtzi, and Akronafplia on the same day, choose a compact model such as a Fiat Panda, Hyundai i10, or Toyota Aygo for easier parking in the old town.

## Rental Car and Insurance Notes for Nafplio

Car rental in Nafplio is typically sold with statutory third-party liability as the legal baseline, while CDW, SCDW, FDW, and WUG are add-ons that may reduce the excess/deductible or franchise. A broker may advertise zero excess or full coverage, but the exact scope should be checked against the rental voucher and the conditions set under Bank of Greece Act 195/3 for payment handling and card preauthorisation practices.

**Common local fleet examples:**

- 🚙 **Economy city cars** — Ford Fiesta, Fiat Panda, Hyundai i10, and Toyota Aygo are common for town parking and short stays.
- 🚗 **Compact hatchbacks** — Peugeot and Citroen models are frequently offered for mixed town-and-highway use.
- 🚐 **Automatic and touring options** — Skoda Octavia automatic, Opel Insignia, Audi, Mercedes, and Nissan are more suitable for longer Argolis routes.
- 🚙 **SUV choice** — Kia Sportage is useful for travelers who want a higher seating position on Peloponnese roads.

> ⚠️ **Warning:** A BAC violation can void insurance under Greek traffic rules, and the police can treat mobile-phone use while driving under Article 17 of the current K.O.K., so the safest choice is to keep your hands free and your alcohol level at zero.

**Useful authorities and local sources:** ELSTAT reports national transport and tourism data; the Hellenic Ministry of Culture at odysseus.culture.gr covers protected sites such as Palamidi Fortress and Akronafplia; the Δήμος Ναυπλιέων publishes municipal parking and street-use guidance; and the Tmima Trochaias Tourismou Nafpliou and Hellenic Police handle road enforcement and accident reporting.

## Nafplio Agencies and Local Names to Know

Several rental brands operate in Nafplio, and naming them explicitly helps when comparing pickup locations, fleet types, and insurance wording before booking.

**Agency references in Nafplio:**

- **Staikos Rent a Car** — rentacarnafplio.gr; owner Christos Bouchoutsos; convenient for Bouboulinas and Syngrou pickups.
- **Bounos Car Rental** — bounos-carrental.com; close to the waterfront and the KTEL terminal at Syngrou 8.
- **Makris Car Rental** — operating since 1986; useful if you want a long-established local option.
- **Alma Car Rental** — almacar.gr; often cited by travelers comparing local pickup quotes.
- **Base Rent a Car** — nafpliorentacar.gr; another Nafplio-local reference point.
- **Euro Rent a Car** — eurorentacar.eu; operating since 1986 and frequently mentioned in the waterfront area.
- **Imperial Car Rental** — appears in broker listings, including published rates such as Ford Fiesta from €18/day, which should be treated as a broker quote rather than a guaranteed local-agency price.
- **Hermes Travel** — a common concierge-style pickup reference near the old town.
- **Stamou Car Rental** — local name to check for short-stay bookings.
- **Aspida Car Rental** — another local agency name that may appear in comparison results.
- **G.A. Car Rental** — linked to owner Mr Gavriil in local listings.
- **Ride & Drive** — a rental brand sometimes used for comparison shopping.

**Local geography and landmarks that affect pickup planning:** Argolis, Peloponnese, Argolic Gulf, Mycenae, Epidaurus, Tiryns, Karathona, Tolo, Porto Heli, Nemea, Asklipiou Avenue, Akti Miaouli, Bouboulinas, Syngrou 8, Palamidi Fortress, Bourtzi, and Akronafplia all appear in rental and route search behavior for Nafplio.

### Internal links to continue planning

- [Nafplio old-town parking guide](/nafplio-old-town-parking)
